name: memorable-date
title: Memorable date
description: A date entered as three separate fields — Month (native <select>), Day, and Year (native numeric text inputs) — under a fieldset + legend. The USWDS memorable-date pattern for dates a user already knows (date of birth, issue date): faster and more accessible than a calendar picker. Hint and error wire at the group level via FieldGroup; focus is never auto-advanced.
useWhen: Collecting a date the user already knows by heart (date of birth, issue or incident date)
A month/day/year answer where typing is faster than navigating a calendar
Forms where a date picker's grid would be slower or less accessible for known dates
avoidWhen: Picking a date from a calendar or browsing availability (use a date picker)
Choosing a start and end date (use a date-range picker)
Dates constrained to a small known set (use a select or radio group)
Times of day (use a time input)

keyboard: Tab moves through Month, Day, then Year in reading order; Shift+Tab reverses
Month is a native <select>: Up/Down Arrow and type-ahead pick a month (platform behavior)
Day and Year are native text inputs with inputmode=numeric; printable digits insert, arrows/Home/End move the caret
Focus is deliberately NOT auto-advanced between fields — the user controls focus, which keeps correction and screen-reader review possible
A disabled group removes all three fields from the tab order via the native fieldset disabled attribute
